---
description: "Use when modifying Ion contract definitions (.ion files), regenerating codegen, or updating interaction implementations after contract changes. Covers ionc CLI usage, breaking change handling, and the correct workflow for contract→codegen→impl updates."
applyTo: "src/Argon.Ion/**"
---
# Ion Codegen Workflow

## Critical Rule

**NEVER manually edit files in `src/Argon.CodeGen/`** — they are 100% auto-generated by `ionc`. Only edit `.ion` source files and `*InteractionImpl.cs` bridge files.

## Workflow

1. Edit `.ion` files in `src/Argon.Ion/`
2. Run `ionc compile -o Dotnet` from the `src/Argon.Ion/` directory
3. If breaking changes are detected → use `ionc compile -o Dotnet --update-lock`
4. Update `*InteractionImpl.cs` in `src/Argon.Core/Services/Ion/` to match new generated interfaces
5. Build and verify

## ionc CLI Reference

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `ionc compile` | Compile all targets (Dotnet + Browser) |
| `ionc compile -o Dotnet` | Compile only .NET codegen |
| `ionc compile --update-lock` | Accept breaking changes, update `ion.lock.json` |
| `ionc compile --check` | Validate only, no code generation |
| `ionc compile --no-lock` | Skip schema lock validation |
| `ionc compile -o Dotnet --update-lock` | Most common: .NET only + accept breaking |

## Project Structure

| Path | Role | Editable? |
|------|------|-----------|
| `src/Argon.Ion/*.ion` | Contract source of truth | YES |
| `src/Argon.Ion/ion.config.json` | Codegen config (output paths, targets) | YES |
| `src/Argon.Ion/ion.lock.json` | Schema lock for breaking change detection | NO (auto-updated) |
| `src/Argon.CodeGen/models/` | Generated DTOs, enums, unions, formatters | NO |
| `src/Argon.CodeGen/server/` | Generated server executors (CBOR→impl) | NO |
| `src/Argon.CodeGen/client/` | Generated client stubs | NO |
| `src/Argon.Core/Services/Ion/*InteractionImpl.cs` | Bridge: generated interface → grain calls | YES |

## After Codegen

The `*InteractionImpl.cs` files implement the generated `I*Interaction` interfaces. After regenerating:
- Check for new/changed method signatures
- Update implementations to match
- These files map Ion RPC calls to Orleans grain method calls
